headline: BREAKING: North Correa Acquires Dustin May and Michael Harris II; Wonder Wharf Wonderdogs Land Randy Arozarena in Midseason Shakeup
body:
Two teams staring at the playoff bubble from the wrong side of it just made a move, and sources familiar with the situation confirm it is official: **Dustin May** and **Michael Harris II** are headed to North Correa, and **Randy Arozarena** is going the other direction to Wonder Wharf Wonderdogs.

North Correa sits 9th at 5-7-2 with 65 points-for. Week 14. The window is not closed, but it is closing. This trade is a survival bid. **May**, when healthy and locked in, is a legitimate ERA and WHIP stabilizer — two categories where a struggling squad can claw back wins against middle-of-the-pack competition. Pair that with IP and QS contributions and North Correa is reshaping its entire pitching profile in one swing. **Harris II** adds a multi-category outfield bat with SB and OBP upside, exactly what a 5-7-2 team needs when it can no longer afford to punt categories. Getting two pieces for one is arithmetic North Correa can live with right now.

Wonder Wharf Wonderdogs, sitting 13th at 3-8-3 and 50 points-for, needed a pulse. **Arozarena** delivers one immediately — SB, R, OBP, the kind of counting-stat producer who can single-handedly swing a weekly matchup. For a team this deep in the standings, volume matters more than depth. They needed one genuinely dangerous bat, and they got it. Whether 3-8-3 is recoverable is a separate, grimmer conversation.

**Winner, short term:** Wonder Wharf Wonderdogs. Arozarena is plug-and-play impact.

**Winner, rest of season:** North Correa. Two assets outperform one, and the pitching categories are where playoff races get decided.
